title: engine.v1 — the shape of the engine manifest
description: The engine manifest is the one committed file that records what this repository's engine is made of: which engine release it runs, the version of each installed module, and the identity tier that governs how guardrail changes are gated. It is a lockfile-style inventory — the source of truth an upgrade reads to know each module's starting version — and doubles as the operator-readable record of the engine's contents. It lives at .engine/engine.json. This schema governs its shape only.

engine_release.description: The engine release this repository runs, as a version string (for example 1.2.0, or 0.0.0-dev while the engine is still being built). This is the single version an operator sees.

packages.description: Each installed module mapped to its installed version (for example { "core": "1.2.0" }). These per-module versions are internal bookkeeping — the 'from' versions an upgrade reads before it overlays new code — not numbers the operator manages directly.

identity.description: The identity tier governing how the engine's own guardrails may change. solo: a single gate-holder — a guardrail can never weaken silently, but a weakening can still happen with the operator's explicit consent (the engine acts with the operator's credentials). team: a distinct engine identity holds enforcement-admin, so a guardrail cannot weaken at all. This is per-repository and re-chosen at first-run setup; it is not inherited from a template.

handle.description: The operator's own account handle, captured at first-run setup and preserved as operator config. It is the owner the engine writes into the code-ownership block for its own files, so an engine update re-renders that block for the new release's files while keeping this owner. Optional: it is absent until first-run setup derives and records it (and absent in the construction repository, which runs no first-run setup), so a manifest without it stays valid.

control_plane.description: What the engine did to this repository's branch protection at setup, recorded so a later clean removal can reverse exactly that and nothing of the operator's. Written by the control-plane apply step on a brownfield arrival (and on greenfield, to record that the engine owns its own ruleset). Optional: absent until the gate is turned on, and on a repo whose protection was already fully in force.

ruleset_mode.description: created: the engine created and owns its own branch-protection ruleset (greenfield). augmented: the engine added its checks into a pre-existing PRODUCT ruleset in place (brownfield), so removal strips back only what it added and never deletes the operator's rule.

augmented_ruleset_id.description: The id of the product ruleset the engine augmented (when ruleset_mode is augmented); null when the engine created its own.

added.description: The exact pieces the engine added to a product ruleset, so removal reverses precisely them: the required-check contexts it unioned in, and any wholly-missing floor rule types it added. null when the engine created its own ruleset.
